item.title: RBC: 2025 Summer - Banking Advisor Student Intern, Manitoba
item.description:
locations
WINNIPEG, Manitoba, Canada
STEINBACH, Manitoba, Canada
BRANDON, Manitoba, Canada
DAUPHIN, Manitoba, Canada
THOMPSON, Manitoba, Canada
View All 7 Locations
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: January 31, 2025 (10 days left to apply)
job requisition id
R-0000109779
Job Summary
Job Description
Are you a fourth-year student and plan to pursue a career in banking? We have Summer Student Intern opportunities available in Manitoba.
What is the opportunity?
Open the door to the first of many opportunities at RBC and experience the challenges and rewards of working in the financial services industry. If you have the drive to deliver exceptional customer service, you can build a great career with us and gain progressive learning opportunities year over year through our Summer Internship Program.
You will gain foundational skills as a Banking Advisor, the face of RBC. You work on the front lines, serving clients, carrying out transactions, solving problems and spotting sales opportunities. This is an important role providing advice to clients and finding solutions that meet their individual needs.
What will you do?
Serve clients by delivering exceptional customer service through completing transactions, engaging in needs-based conversations, and communicating with clients throughout the branch
Identify needs and provide tailored financial advice and solutions to clients, when possible, at the point of first contact
Contribute to team sales results by listening and spotting opportunities to offer additional advice, introduce clients to the capability of RBC partners, or personally fulfil client solutions
Understand & resolve client concerns, where possible, at first point of contact
Educate & assist clients with using RBC digital capabilities
Manage risks by adhering to compliance routines, processes, and controls to protect client and shareholder interests while completing transactions
What do you need to succeed?
Must-have
Proven customer service skills
Drive and self-motivation, as well as excellent communication skills and emotional intelligence
Digital literacy across a broad range of devices (i.e., smartphones, tablets, laptops, etc.)
Personal flexibility to work a variety of hours including evenings and weekends- Eagerness to learn and determination to succeed
Nice-to-have
Sales experience in the financial or service industry
What’s in it for you?
We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.
Excellent career development and the potential to return each summer for progressive opportunities until graduation
A world-class training program in financial services
A comprehensive Total Rewards Program and competitive compensation- Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Join our Talent Community
Stay in-the-know about great career opportunities at RBC. Sign up and get customized info on our latest jobs, career tips and Recruitment events that matter to you. Expand your limits and create a new future together at RBC. Find out how we use our passion and drive to enhance the well-being of our clients and communities at jobs.rbc.com
Job Skills
Customer Experience (CX), Customer Service, Personal Initiative, Teamwork
Additional Job Details
Address:
220 PORTAGE AVE:WINNIPEG
City:
WINNIPEG
Country:
Canada
Work hours/week:
37.5
Employment Type:
Full time
Platform:
Job Type:
Student/Coop (Fixed Term)
Pay Type:
Salaried
Posted Date:
2024-12-20
Application Deadline:
2025-01-31
Note: Applications will be accepted until 11:59 PM on the day prior to the application deadline date above
Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment
At RBC, we embrace diversity and inclusion for innovation and growth. We are committed to building inclusive teams and an equitable workplace for our employees to bring their true selves to work. We are taking actions to tackle issues of inequity and systemic bias to support our diverse talent, clients and communities.

We also strive to provide an accessible candidate experience for our prospective employees with different abilities. Please let us know if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process.
Join our Talent Community

Stay in-the-know about great career opportunities at RBC. Sign up and get customized info on our latest jobs, career tips and Recruitment events that matter to you.

Expand your limits and create a new future together at RBC. Find out how we use our passion and drive to enhance the well-being of our clients and communities at jobs.rbc.com.

item.title: CIBC: People, Culture and Brand Coordinator - Co-Op
item.description:
locations
Toronto, ON
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 3, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
2501486
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What You’ll Be Doing

As a Coordinator in People, Culture and Brand Co-op, you will manage the day-to-day coordinator activities in support of your team. Opportunities exist in Inclusion, Workplace Design and Architecture ,Total Rewards, Talent Development, Corporate Events and Marketing. Responsibilities also include on-going administrative coordination in support of our People leaders and their teams. ensuring timely and effective delivery of assigned projects. Provide technical, administrative, and any project coordination support to the team you are assigned to.  At this level, you are expected to function independently with very little direction, seek help and clarification as required, as well as possess strong initiative, and ability to foresee potential issues/problems that may arise in support of the Leaders or teams.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.
Important Information
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
You must be currently enrolled in post-secondary education and returning to full-time studies to be eligible. However, you do not need to be in a registered Co-Op program to be considered for a role.
Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded into the Resume section of the application as one document.
You have a strong knowledge of MS Office - Word, Excel, PowerPoint.
Work Location: Further clarity on the work location will be provided at the time of your interview. Please note all employees must work within their current jurisdiction and must reside within Canada. All CIBC Co-op positions require you to be based in Canada for the duration of the term.
Please note, we have multiple positions available under this posting and you may be considered by more than one hiring team.
How You’ll Succeed
Strong communication and interpersonal skills -.  Interact with a wide range of individuals through different communication channels, which requires tact, diplomacy, and discretion.
Strong initiative and self-managing skills -  to be able to work independently with very little direction and to respond effectively to issues as well as potential issues that may arise. 
You know that details matter. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
Project Support and Partnerships - Liaise with internal sources and business partners. Work with team members to support activities and overall project planning and execution through various campaigns.
Advanced technical skills -  in software packages such as Microsoft Word, Power Point, Excel, Outlook, SharePoint, desktop publishing etc., as well as various types of office equipment.
Who You Are
Client-Focused. You put our clients first. You engage with purpose to find the right solutions. You go the extra mile, because it's the right thing to do.
You're digitally savvy. You seek out innovative solutions and embrace evolving technologies. You can easily adapt to new tools and trends.
You're passionate about people. You find meaning in relationships, and surround yourself with a diverse network of partners. You build trust through respect and authenticity.
You're driven by collective success. You know that collaboration can transform a good idea into a great one. You understand the power of an inclusive team that enjoys working together to bring a shared vision to life.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability.
You are on your path to complete a degree/diploma in Business, Marketing HR or related f.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
*Subject to program terms and conditions
What you need to know
CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
Expected End Date
2025-08-22
Job Location
Toronto-81 Bay, 33rd Floor
Employment Type
Temporary (Fixed Term)
Weekly Hours
37.5
Skills
Business, Collaboration, Decision Making, Interpersonal Communication

item.title: CIBC: Universal Fraud Agent - Remote - Bilingual
item.description:
locations
Montreal-1155 Rene Levesque
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 7, 2025 (17 days left to apply)
job requisition id
2500067
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What you'll be doing 
As a member of the Fraud Contact Centre team, a Fraud Agent will be responsible for ensuring the delivery of service to both external and internal customers consistent with CIBC’s vision of winning customer loyalty through service excellence. You will identify, analyze and conduct investigations of client transactions through credit cards and debit cards. You will use other banking channels to determine possible fraud or unusual activity while mitigating fraud losses to CIBC clients. You’ll use extensive analytical ability, resolve all complex or difficult problems relating to Fraud Applications, Account Takeovers, Convenience Cheques, and Payments referred by other areas of CIBC or other external sources as well as from our monitoring systems.
Please note that the start date is March 17th, 2025 and it's a full time permanent position. A quick note on your availability – our Contact Centre is open Monday to Sunday, 7:00 am to midnight. We’d like for you to be flexible between these hours.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You can effectively perform all work activities remotely, and will only be required to be on-site on an occasional basis.
We have embarked on an Agent@Home remote work program. All employees are required to meet the Agent@Home program requirements and be able to attend your primary work location within two hours’ notice for any business-related purposes.
Agent@Home Program Requirements:
Ability to work independently as an Agent@Home with a secure workspace, in a room or office with a door that closes it off from noises and distractions.
Wired Internet connection, capable of continuously supporting excellent call quality and high-speed response rate (Internet connection can be connected to modem through hard cable (not wireless) with a minimum download speed of 15 mbps and upload speed of 10 mbps)
Satellite internet providers are prohibited and cannot be used due to the impact on call quality and the stability of service
How you'll succeed 
Investigation- Identify suspected fraudulent activities using knowledge of risk management principles, policies and procedures to mitigate fraud and minimize losses to CIBC & in an efficient and timely manner to meet productivity goals.
Analysis – Use sound decision making skills to react quickly to incoming calls/referrals and make sound decisions in situations where limited information/ambiguity exists including reviewing customer’s profiles, transactions and banking history to minimize exposure to loss, and protect CIBC and our clients privacy
Collaboration – Identify, refer and escalate different types of fraud to relevant areas within the Identity Theft Response Fraud Contact Center to, Fraud Strategy, Corporate Security, Loss Management, Client Care, Ombudsman Office, VISA, and Intria) to be effectively investigated and resolved.
Who you are 
You can demonstrate experience in COINS/ECIF, PRM, TSYS and CRS is an asset.
You put our clients first. You engage with purpose to find the right solutions. You go the extra mile, because it's the right thing to do.
You understand that success is in the details. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making
You act like an owner. You thrive when you're empowered to take the lead, go above and beyond and deliver results
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability
You're fluent in French and English to serve our clients in the community.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential. We aspire to give you a career, rather than just a paycheck.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive salary, incentive pay, banking benefits, a benefits program*, defined benefit pension plan*, an employee share purchase plan, a vacation offering, wellbeing support, and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
We cultivate a culture where you can express your ambition through initiatives like Purpose Day; a paid day off dedicated for you to use to invest in your growth and development.
*Subject to plan and program terms and conditions
What you need to know
CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
Job Location
Montreal-1155 Rene Levesque
Employment Type
Regular
Weekly Hours
37.5
Skills
Call Center, Collaboration, Credit, Customer Service, Teamwork

item.title: CIBC: Employee Engagement Co-Op (8 months)
item.description:
locations
Toronto, ON
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 3, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
2501479
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What You’ll Be Doing

Reporting to the Director, Employee Engagement & Partnerships and Senior Manager, Employee Engagement & Partnerships the incumbent will be responsible for supporting the development, implementation, execution and maintenance of CIBC’s One for Change employee giving and volunteering program and various charity partners relationships and campaigns. The role will support community partnerships, and associated employee engagement and client opportunities across all areas of CIBC with operational excellence, efficiencies, consistent branding and professionalism.

The Employee Engagement Co-op is responsible for engaging employees and community partners in the journey to drive employee pride and consideration for our organization. Responsible for deepening relationships with employees and community partners through simplified experiences and creating frictionless programs to build momentum and inspire internal culture and external praise. This role supports CIBC’s ambitions to be a good corporate citizen and drive community and social impact by developing deep and mutually beneficial relationships with community partners and charities.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.
Important Information
Duration of term: 8 month opportunity (May 2025 – December 2025).
You must be currently enrolled in post-secondary education and returning to full-time studies to be eligible. However, you do not need to be in a registered Co-op program to be considered for this role.
You are a university student currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program.
You have a strong knowledge of MS Office – PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook, Word.
You must be legally eligible to work within Canada.
Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded as a single pdf document.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.


How You’ll Succeed


Administration - Manage administrative activities in support of the Employee Engagement & Partnerships team using independent judgment, confidentiality and diplomacy. Support the facilitation, organization, planning, relationships, tracking and monitoring of the CIBC Volunteer Program. Support corporate grant making by tracking, reporting and using internal grants database. Lastly, participate in external research on corporate social responsibility, ESG and fundraising best practices and trends
Communication - Draft internal social media content supporting charitable partnership and the One for Change employee giving and volunteering program
Tracking, Reporting and Analysis - this role will be responsible for managing and analyzing the reporting/tracking of whichever campaigns and key relationships he/she/they supports throughout the duration of the term
Project Support and Partnerships - Liaise with internal sources, business partners, and third parties as required. Work with team members to support activities and overall project plan/delivery for key campaigns.
Who You Are
You're motivated by collective success. You know that teamwork can transform a good idea into a great one. You know that an inclusive team that enjoys working together can bring a vision to life
You know that details matter. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
It’s an asset if you have previous sales or fundraising skills with proven track record of creatively engaging stakeholders, tracking and analyzing fundraising/giving results and development of mitigation plans when necessary considered an asset.
You know that details matter. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills -.  Interact with a wide range of individuals through different communication channels, which requires tact, diplomacy, and discretion.
You’re goal oriented. You’re motivated by accomplishing your goals and delivering your best to make a difference.
You are fluent in Microsoft Office (Word, PowerPoint, Excel).  Experience working with PowerPoint, Excel, Word and Outlook is an asset.


WORKING CONDITIONS
• This role operates within a normal office environment with little exposure to adverse working conditions.

COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS/RESPONSIBILITIES
• As an employee of CIBC, the incumbent must comply with all applicable CIBC and Line of Business policies, standards, guidelines and controls.

AUTHORITIES/DECISION RIGHTS
• As a key contributor to the business unit, this job has the authority to recommend changes to business processes in order to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
*Subject to program terms and conditions
What you need to know
CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
Expected End Date
2025-08-22
Job Location
Toronto-81 Bay, 33rd Floor
Employment Type
Temporary (Fixed Term)
Weekly Hours
37.5
Skills
Business, Communication, Decision Making, Employee Engagement

item.title: CIBC: Associate, Communications Co-Op
item.description:
locations
Toronto, ON
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 3, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
2501474
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What You'll Be Doing
The Associate, Communications will be a key member of our Communications & Public Affairs team and is responsible for coordinating internal or external communications and supporting key operational and administrative activities in support of our executive members. The role applies basic theories to accomplish small-scope assignments, following detailed instructions and documented procedures. The Associate, Communications uses judgement to manage priorities and approach, providing support on communications initiatives and resolving basic issues.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.
Important Information
Duration of term: Summer 2025 (May 2025 – September 2025).
You must be currently enrolled in post-secondary education and returning to full-time studies to be eligible. However, you do not need to be in a registered Co-op program to be considered for this role.
You are a university student currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program.
You have a strong knowledge of MS Office – PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook, Word.
You must be legally eligible to work within Canada.
Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded as a single pdf document.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
How You’ll Succeed
Strong communication and interpersonal skills -  to interact with a wide range of individuals in a wide range of situations, which usually requires tact, diplomacy, and discretion.
Strong initiative and self-managing skills -  to be able to work independently with very little direction and to respond effectively to issues as well as potential issues that may arise.
Advanced technical skills -  in software packages such as Microsoft Word, Power Point, Excel, Outlook, SharePoint, desktop publishing etc., as well as various types of office equipment.
Decisiveness and good judgement - required to effectively operate the executive office, typically in the executive’s absence.
Who You Are
You know that details matter. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
You act like an owner. You thrive when you’re empowered to take the lead, go above and beyond, and deliver results.
You put our clients first. You engage with purpose to find the right solutions. You go the extra mile, because it's the right thing to do.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.

We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What You’ll Be Doing
The Web Publisher – Co-op is a member of the Corporate Intranet team which is responsible for updating and enhancing our corporate and social intranet sites. You will be accountable for publishing pages, documents, and images to CIBC intranet. This includes receiving, preparing, publishing and reviewing content changes while adhering to corporate standards and brand guidelines to all intranet contents. This individual will assess requests for compliance and will proactively identify possible compliance issues under the intranet governance model.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.
Important Information
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.

Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded into the Resume section of the application as one document.
You have a strong knowledge of MS Office - Word, Excel, PowerPoint.
Work Location: Further clarity on the work location will be provided at the time of your interview. Please note all employees must work within their current jurisdiction and must reside within Canada. All CIBC Co-op positions require you to be based in Canada for the duration of the term.
Duration of Term : Summer 2025 (May – August)
How You’ll Succeed
Publish Content – Work closely with members of the Intranet team, business partners, and communications team to prepare and publish intranet contents. Ensure that urgent news messages and escalations are managed in a timely manner.
Quality Assurance and Auditing - Ensure brand guidelines are applied to all intranet contents. Ensure deliverables are reviewed for quality assurance, prior to business partner reviews or publishing.
Organization – Demonstrate ability to prioritize tasks, calendar and emails in an efficient and effective manner. Time management is critical in this role
Who You Are
You're digitally savvy. You can demonstrate understanding of Internet/Intranet techniques, design, and information architecture as well as working knowledge of Microsoft Suite. Experience in data entry and graphic design using Adobe Photoshop and HTML is an asset.
You embrace and advocate for change. You continuously evolve your thinking and the way you work in order to deliver your best.

item.description:
locations
Toronto, ON
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 3, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
2501464
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What You’ll Be Doing
You will be joining an outstanding team working in Enterprise Real Estate and Workplace Transformation (ERE & WT). Enterprise Real Estate Strategy charts the strategic direction of CIBC’s workplaces globally. The team is responsible for the development of premises strategies in major regional centers to improve employee and client experiences, manage capital investment and operating expenses, while achieving CIBC’s purpose and ESG goals. We bring together research, data and thought leadership to generate a long-term perspective for our workplaces.

As the Corporate Real Estate Strategy Co-Op, your key focus will be to support the team, utilizing your experience and knowledge to advance strategic planning activities and the creation of standardized delivery processes and toolsets.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.
Important Information
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
You must be currently enrolled in post-secondary education and returning to full-time studies to be eligible. However, you do not need to be in a registered Co-Op program to be considered for a role.
Recruitment timeline: Applications review – January; Interviews – February/March; Offers – March/April
Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded into the Resume section of the application as one pdf document
Duration of Term: Summer 2025 (May - August)


How you’ll succeed
Strategic Planning - Corporate Real Estate Initiatives – working closely with the real-estate strategy lead and subject-matter experts, you will help envision the future, shape and support advancement of CIBC’s premises strategies.
Analytical & Financial acumen –You have experience completing financial analysis and are aware of key financial considerations of investment decisions. This foundational knowledge will help you gather information from multiple sources, analyze, synthesize and visualize your insights in a meaningful manner.
Communication – Your strong oral and written communication skills will ensure internal partners are clear on objectives and have a sufficient level of detail. You will assist with the preparation of reports and decks. These artifacts will be presented to a variety of audiences and assist senior stakeholders in decisioning.
Organization – You will provide administrative support by scheduling meetings, taking and distributing notes and following up in critical items in a timely manner.
Process Improvement – You will contribute to the development of procedures, tool and templates including the design and preparation of these toolsets to ensure operational excellence and efficiency.
Collaboration – You will have exposure to various stakeholders within the organization. Your ability to build relationships through trust, proactivity and the anticipation of your partner’s needs will help you succeed.

Who you are
You demonstrate an understanding of real-estate and construction industries. You’ll spend time researching key industry trends that will help envision the future.
You act like an owner. You thrive when you’re empowered to take the lead, go above and beyond and deliver results. You maximize value for the organization.
You give meaning to data. You investigate and solve complex problems and are able to make sense of information. You are confident and able to communicate detailed information in an impactful way.
You’re digitally savvy. You seek out innovative solutions and embrace evolving technology. You easily adapt to new tools and trends. You demonstrate proficiency with business intelligence tools (Tableau, Power BI) and Microsoft Office Suite (MS Teams, Power Point, Excel, Visio, MS Forms).
You understand that success is in the details. You notice things that other’s don’t. Your critical thinking skills help inform your decision making.
You’re driven by collective success. You know that collaboration can transform a good idea into a great one. You understand the power of an inclusive team that enjoys working together to bring a shared vision to life.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.

CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com

The Co-op Archives Assistant will and arrange and describe unprocessed digital and physical accessions, help inventory and photograph the Archives’ artefact collection and support the migration of the Archives’ database to a new content management system.

Arrange and describe, re-house and update database records for un-processed physical material
Process and index digital collections

Who You Are
You are pursuing a graduate program in Archives or related discipline.

Your day to day will be to assist with the idea generation and analysis to identify skill gaps and opportunities, work on various projects across the team, and supporting the development and delivery of training to the frontline Imperial Service advisory team. You will help contribute to process improvements and enhancements to the internal sites and resources.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You’ll have the flexibility to manage your work activities within a hybrid work arrangement where you’ll spend 1-3 days per week on-site, while other days will be remote.

Duration of term: Summer 2025 (May 2025 - August 2025)
How you'll succeed
Client engagement - Connect on a personal level to make every interaction meaningful. Enhance relationships by collaborating with your team and providing administrative support to ensure client needs are met. Respond to requests by answering questions and providing an exceptional experience.
Time Management - Ensure timely production and distribution of performance reports and statements of net worth.
Relationship building - Work closely with your team to connect clients with the right opportunities.
Building efficiencies - Driving continuous improvement of operational policies and procedures supporting various other activities.
Innovation - Make an impact by assisting in problem solving and finding solution and often going above and beyond in assisting to find a resolution.
Developing and enhancing - You'll increase both the due diligence process and ongoing monitoring of sophisticated investment strategies around the globe, primarily via automation and optimization.
Who you are
You know that details matter. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
You put our clients first. You engage with purpose to find the right solutions. You go the extra mile, because it's the right thing to do.
You love to learn. You're passionate about growing your knowledge. You have a strong sense of curiosity.
You have advanced communication and interpersonal skills. You have the ability to interact with people at all levels.
You're driven to succeed. You are motivated by accomplishing your goals and delivering your best to make an impact.
You are working towards a degree/diploma. You are working towards a Bachelor’s degree in a business-related discipline and/or knowledge of business, economics and/or finance through education and experience.

As an Analyst, Digital Strategy (Co-op), you are accountable for supporting the development of digital solutions to enable digital acquisition, onboarding and conversion through CIBC’s Online & Mobile Banking platforms. You're passionate about the intersection of business, design and technology and strive to make banking at CIBC a leading digital experience for our clients.

· Market Research: Proactively scan the market to identify and assess new and emerging digital capabilities and CIBC Digital’s competitive standing.
· Development of Communication Materials: Provide and update materials for stakeholder presentations, summaries of competitive intelligence and KPI tracking.
· Support Roadmap Development: Help develop business cases, quantify financial impact of projects, and assist with project prioritization.
· Cross-Functional Collaboration: Create strong relationships across Strategy, Innovation, Product Delivery, and other lines of businesses to provide functional insights for strategic projects.

We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

What you’ll be doing
As a member of the Sales Origination Product Delivery team, you will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing frontline digital products with capabilities that address business objectives and enable optimal client experience. As a Digital Product Specialist Co-op on the frontline sales origination platform, you will assist the product team in delivering the product iterations that satisfies user, client and business partner needs while maximizing business value. This role will be assigned to delivery pod(s) that focus on product and/or project delivery impacting our sales and origination platform, leveraged by our frontline employees across CIBC’s channels.
In this role, you will assist the product team in being the voice of the user and prioritizing features & deliverables. You’ll support the delivery pod in understanding the needs of various stakeholders (lines of business, technology, risk, governance etc.), in ensuring alignment across various groups regarding the product iterations, and ensuring that the team understands, and is aligned to the vision and path of delivering the product iteration. You’ll also identify and document key processes and procedures impacting your delivery pod.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You’ll have the flexibility to manage your work activities within a hybrid work arrangement where you’ll spend 1-3 days per week on-site, while other days will be remote.
Important Information
· We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
· You must be currently enrolled in post-secondary education and returning to full-time studies to be eligible. However, you do not need to be in a registered Co-Op program to be considered for a role.
· Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded into the Resume section of the application as one pdf document.
· You are a university student pursuing a Bachelor’s degree. Accounting, Finance, Business, Economics major is an asset, but not required.
· Further clarity on the work location will be provided at the time of your interview. Please note all employees must work within their current jurisdiction and must reside within Canada. All CIBC Co-op positions require you to be based in Canada for the duration of the term.
· Duration of term: 8 months (May 2025 – Dec 2025)
· Please note, we have multiple positions available under this posting and you may be considered by more than one hiring team.
How You’ll Succeed
· Support the product team with key deliverables – working with stakeholders, business partners, and analytics to understand the business/user/customer problem and develop a product backlog that solves for user problems, meets business objectives and prioritizes work with the maximum value. Support in determining/analyzing success metrics & benefits.
· Identify & document key processes and procedures – working closely with the product team and key stakeholders, serve as process subject matter expert for the assigned product, projects and/or initiatives.
· Assist with user stories & product requirement creation – support product team in the development user stories in a collaborative fashion with business system analysts, design, technology teams, lines of business and various members of the product team.
· Drive alignment with stakeholders – assist product team in ensuring that we are delivering the right initiatives at the right time and keeping our stakeholders engaged throughout the delivery life-cycle. Understand and support the team in managing competing needs & priorities.
Who You Are
· You're digitally savvy. You seek out innovative solutions and embrace evolving technologies. You can easily adapt to new tools and trends.
· You understand that success is in the details. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
· You're driven by collective success. You know that collaboration can transform a good idea into a great one. You know that relationships and networks are essential to success. You inspire outcomes by making yourself heard and by surround yourself with a diverse network of partners. You build trust through respect, authenticity.
· You act like an owner. You thrive when you're empowered to take the lead, go above and beyond, and deliver results.
· You can effectively communicate. Your communication is effective and reflective based on who your audience is. You can foster buy-in with stakeholders and partners.
· Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability.

What you’ll be doing:

As a Business Analyst on the Real Estate Secured Lending (RESL) Strategy team, you will be part of the Personal Lending group, which encompasses mortgages, secured and unsecured lending, and auto lending products. Your responsibilities will include establishing, implementing, and maintaining ongoing business processes to ensure alignment with our strategic goals. In addition, you will create and facilitate internal communications, such as leadership reports, strategy documents, town halls, and executive meetings presentations, in support of the Senior Directors, VP, and the overall business unit.

General Strategy and Business Management Support – Provides support for the strategic and reporting needs of the RESL team, including the creation of strategic presentations and analytical reports. Maintains a coordinating role for the leadership team regarding critical meetings and events.
Strategic Communication – Demonstrates well-developed written and verbal communication skills, including with senior leadership. Ability to produce a variety of business documents that adhere to best practices in strategic communication, ensuring clarity, coherence, and alignment with organizational goals. Capable of articulating both factual and conceptual information that requires detailed explanation or interpretation with effective storytelling. Skilled in simplifying complex concepts into easy-to-understand formats that resonate with an executive audience, facilitating informed decision-making.
Cross Functional Relationships – Interacts and collaborates regularly, as a critical function, with Personal lending leadership team and their representatives. Develops and leverages the relationships, and uses considerable influence to achieve outcomes, while improving cross-group relationships. Interacts regularly with various internal LOBs and departments to accomplish administrative or coordination tasks, and with other advisory or support units when matters arise that require their involvement.
Communication – Well developed written & verbal communication skills, including with senior leadership.  Ability to produce a ariety of business documents with clarity of thought and orderliness of presentation.  Ability to articulate factual and conceptual information which require detailed explanation or interpretation. 
Relationship Management – Comfortable with reaching out to others across the bank to find answers quickly.  Making and maintaining connections across the bank eeffectively.  Communicating with ease when coordinating complex events with partners from various groups, and with senior leadership.
Analytical Skills – Possesses analytical abilities (proficiency in Excel is a strong asset) to conduct data analysis and track business performance metrics. Capable of interpreting data sets, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to inform strategic decision-making. Demonstrates attention to detail and the ability to present findings in a clear and concise manner, facilitating effective communication with stakeholders.
Who you are
You are a dynamic individual who thrives in collaborative environments and embodies the values of trust, teamwork, and accountability. Your diverse skill set includes:
Problem Solving. You enjoy being presented with problems or issues and working with a team to find innovative solutions
Communication.  You have excellent verbal and written communication skills to effectively articulate ideas and opinions that involve research, interpretation, and assessment.
Process Improvement. You enjoy the process of reviewing current processes and identifying improvements that will optimize or improve standards
Multitasking & Organizational Skills. You possess strong organizational abilities and thrive in fast-paced environments and excel at juggling multiple responsibilities simultaneously. Your ability to prioritize tasks and maintain focus allows you to deliver high-quality results, even under tight deadlines.
Eagerness to Learn. You have a strong desire and enthusiasm to continuously learn and grow, actively seeking out new challenges and opportunities for professional development.
Teamwork. You care about people and understand different perspectives. You work well with others and learn from the experiences of others.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values – trust, teamwork and accountability
If you are passionate about making a difference and are ready to contribute to our dynamic team, we invite you to apply! We look forward to learning more about you and how you can help shape the future of RESL at CIBC.

item.description:
locations
Toronto, ON

2501378
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
Job Description
What you’ll be doing
CIBC’s Deposits Business team has ownership of a wide range of products within Personal and Business Banking, including GICs, Everyday Savings, Everyday Banking and Payments with a focus on Client Acquisition, Enablement and Success. Our mandate includes end to end oversight of product development, business development, campaign implementation, client relationship management and managing the P/L.
As a Product Consultant, you will have the unique opportunity to contribute to our dynamic team's efforts in the fast-paced world of deposit products. This position offers exposure to various aspects of the personal deposits industry, including campaign development, product management, reporting, and innovation. You will play a vital role in supporting our team's mission to optimize payment processes, enhance industry relationships, and drive operational excellence.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.
Important Information
You must be currently enrolled in post-graduate education to be eligible, however, you do not need to be in a registered Co-Op program to be considered for this role
Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded into the Resume section of the application as one pdf document
You are a university student pursuing a Masters degree. Accounting, Finance, Business, Economics major is an asset, but not required
Further clarity on the work location will be provided at the time of your interview. Please note all employees must work within their current jurisdiction and must reside within Canada

We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
Please note, we have multiple positions available under this posting and you may be considered by more than one hiring team
How You’ll Succeed
Reporting - Assist in the preparation of reports and presentations for senior management, providing updates on team activities and industry insights.
Industry Research - Conduct research on payment industry trends, benchmarks, and emerging technologies to provide insights and recommendations. Perform deep dives with regards to penetration, attrition, priority segments performance and areas of opportunity.
Innovation Initiatives - Collaborate with team members on innovation projects, helping to implement new solutions and technologies.
Performance Monitoring - Assist with product launches and monitor campaign performance, review existing portfolios and provide recommendations for optimization.
Problem solving - You take initiative to develop creative solutions. You engage with team members and business partners to achieve common goals. You are able to work with business stakeholders to gather requirements and develop a deeper understanding of the bank’s processes.
Strong Communication skills - You have well developed interpersonal skills. You are able to take complex concepts and present them in easy to understand written and graphical formats, utilizing spreadsheets, presentation software and web tools. You present your insights, translating them into meaningful observations for varying audience types. You have strong verbal and presentation skills.
Adaptive - You are able to work independently with guidance from management and also in a dynamic team environment.
Cross Collaboration - You engage with team members and business partners to achieve common goals. You are able to work with business stakeholders to gather requirements and develop a deeper understanding of the bank’s processes.
Who You Are
You embrace and champion change. You'll continuously evolve your thinking and the way you work in order to deliver your best
You act like an owner. You thrive when you're empowered to take the lead, go above and beyond, and deliver results
You can demonstrate experience in analytics. It’s an asset if you have experience Analytics, storyboarding and/or executive level communications.
You're driven by collective success. You know that collaboration can transform a good idea into a great one. You understand the power of an inclusive team that enjoys working together to bring a shared vision to life
You give meaning to data. You enjoy investigating complex problems, and making sense of information. You're confident in your ability to communicate detailed information in an impactful way
You're passionate about people. You find meaning in relationships, and surround yourself with a diverse network of partners. You build trust through respect and authenticity

item.title: CIBC: Contact Centre Representative (Mutual Funds, Remote) - Bilingual
item.description:
locations
Montréal, QC
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 28, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
2500377
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.
At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.
To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com
What You’ll Be Doing
You will join the Contact Centre Investment Team as a Mutual Funds Representative. You will focus on providing an excellent client experience, by recognizing opportunities to promote CIBC investment products and services that help clients achieve their financial goals. You will be responding to client inquiries related to mutual fund and fixed term investments and provide detailed and in-depth information on products and services.
The start date for this role is April 7th, 2025. Our Contact Centre is open Monday to Friday from 08:00 AM to 08:00 PM. This is a full-time and shift oriented role. We would like for you to be flexible between these hours.
At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You can effectively perform all work activities remotely, and will only be required to be on-site on an occasional basis.
We have embarked on an Agent@Home remote work program. All employees are required to meet the Agent@Home program requirements and be able to attend your primary work location within two hours’ notice for any business related purposes.
Agent @ Home Program Requirements
Ability to work independently in a private and quiet workspace (ie. in a room or office with a door that closes)
Wired Internet connection capable of continuously supporting excellent call quality and high-speed response rate (internet connection can be connected to modem through hard cable (not wireless) with a minimum download speed of 15 mbps and upload speed of 10 mbps)
Satellite internet providers are prohibited and cannot be used due to the impact on call quality and the stability of service.
How You’ll Succeed
Client Engagement – Build rapport and establish positive working relationships with clients by using discovery questions and industry standard Know Your Client (KYC) questions to determine client needs and provide information on the right investment products or services.
Product Knowledge - Provide detailed, factual and/or technical information on Savings, Income, Growth, Index and Managed Portfolio Solutions for various Mutual Fund account types. Open new Mutual Funds accounts and complete Mutual Fund transactions on existing accounts, including purchases, redemptions and transfers (buying and selling) from one fund to another by following all CIRO rules and regulations and completing a KYC before each interaction.
Problem Solving - Listen, ask questions, and put yourself in the client’s shoes. Act like an owner by taking accountability for client issues, and know when to lean on others to find the right solutions. Recognize and act on opportunities to engage clients in discussions of additional CIBC products and services.
Who You Are
You put our clients first. You engage with purpose to find the right solutions. You go the extra mile, because it’s the right thing to do.
You’re driven to succeed. You are motivated by accomplishing your goals and delivering your best to make an impact.
You give meaning to data. You enjoy investigating complex problems, and making sense of information. You're confident in your ability to communicate detailed information in an impactful way.
You’re a certified professional (CIRO). You have current accreditation and remain in good standing in The Canadian Securities Course (CSC)/Canadian Investments Funds Course (CIFC) on an ongoing basis and must meet eligibility requirements for CIRO licensing.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.
You’re fluent in French and English to support business operations outside of Quebec, Canada.
What CIBC Offers
At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential. We aspire to give you a career, rather than just a paycheck.
We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive salary, incentive pay, banking benefits, a benefits program*, defined benefit pension plan*, an employee share purchase plan, a vacation offering, wellbeing support, and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
We cultivate a culture where you can express your ambition through initiatives like Purpose Day; a paid day off dedicated for you to use to invest in your growth and development.
*Subject to plan and program terms and conditions
What you need to know
CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.

Montreal-1155 Rene Levesque

Regular
Weekly Hours
37.5

Call Center, Client Service, Communication, Inbound Calls, Mutual Funds, Telephone Banking, Work Collaboratively

Personal and Business Banking provides a full range of financial products and services to almost 11 million personal, business and wealth clients, as well as investment management services globally to retail and institutional clients.
As a Cards Acquisition Analyst Co-op, you will be responsible for supporting key client acquisition and revenue enhancement strategies including: analyzing and optimizing existing programs, managing and monitoring key business metrics, analyzing and developing new partnership opportunities and identifying opportunities within existing processes and systems for improvement and optimization. item.title: Manulife: Service Relationship Specialist
item.description:
locations
Vancouver, British Columbia
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 5, 2025 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010200
Manulife is seeking a highly motivated and expert Service Relationship Specialist to support our Group Benefits division. This role is integral in supporting distribution partners, advising on benefit plans, and educating clients. The ideal candidate will possess strong relationship-building skills, technical expertise, and the ability to handle multiple priorities.
Key Responsibilities:
Support – 35%
New Business Acquisition:
Support distribution partners in acquiring new business.
Participate and present in finalist presentations and implementation meetings for mid-market segments.
Serve as a liaison and subject-matter guide in short-list presentations and ongoing meetings for implementing new business.
Client Inquiries and Escalations:
Respond to benefit plan inquiries and resolve raised and complex issues from Plan Sponsors, Plan Advisors/Consultants, and internal contacts.
Lead the unique demands of each customer, ensuring thorough and timely responses.
Service Resolution:
Champion successful outcomes for all involved, handling and negotiating service critical issues.
Advocate for favorable conclusions for all parties involved.
Amendment Requests:
Initiate amendment requests, ensuring information is complete and outstanding requirements are obtained timely.
Collaborate with Customer Experience/Operations teams to facilitate information-sharing and identify key issues.
Internal Initiatives and Meetings:
Assist with general internal initiatives related to Group Benefits services, products, and procedures.
Facilitate multiple meetings with Plan Advisors/Consultants, Plan Sponsors, and internal teams.
Take on special projects and research assignments for client needs.
Advise – 35%
Relationship Building:
Build and maintain successful and lasting relationships with existing and potential partners.
Understand the intricacies and complexities of each Plan Sponsor's Group Benefits designs and the sophistication of the Advisors/Consulting houses.
Client Reporting and Analysis:
Prepare, analyze, and interpret client reports.
Generate and compare client reports using web analytics platforms to ensure client needs are met.
Digital Expertise:
Provide expertise on Manulife’s digital experience for group benefits clients (web, mobile, voice).
Highlight key features and functionality, driving adoption of digital tools and experiences among plan members.
Provide reporting on digital uptake/usage to help clients understand overall plan health and identify areas for education/engagement.
Plan Design and Administration:
Provide recommendations on plan design, service options, technology solutions, and ongoing administration needs.
Apply understanding of legislative and privacy guidelines in client meetings.
Educate – 30%
Presentations and Training:
Deliver presentations to employee groups to facilitate enrollment and introduce Group Benefits (in person or remote).
Meet with plan administrators to train and educate them on effective plan administration methods.
Lead customer workshops and training around product updates and new features.
Product Launches:
Collaborate with Account Executives to learn, present, and educate clients on new services and products.
Use Seismic Tools to create presentations and deliver services to clients.
Assist clients in handling new services, products, and legislative initiatives.
Coaching and Mentoring:
Employ a coaching mentality to define solutions and deliver outcomes.
What are we looking for :
Must have a valid driver’s license and a personal automobile.
Capability to resolve sensitive client needs or issues.
Passionate about nurturing positive relationships and skilled at balancing multiple priorities.
Outstanding analytical, strategic, and problem-solving thinking ability.
Ability to work independently and in a team setting, with innovative thinking and a keen eye for results.
Strong customer-facing skills, including expectation management and communication.

item.title: Manulife: Service Relationship Specialist
item.description:
locations
Winnipeg, Manitoba
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 5, 2025 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010613
Manulife is seeking a highly motivated and expert Service Relationship Specialist to support our Group Benefits division. This role is integral in supporting distribution partners, advising on benefit plans, and educating clients. The ideal candidate will possess strong relationship-building skills, technical expertise, and the ability to handle multiple priorities.
Key Responsibilities:
Support – 35%
New Business Acquisition:
Support distribution partners in acquiring new business.
Participate and present in finalist presentations and implementation meetings for mid-market segments.
Serve as a liaison and subject-matter guide in short-list presentations and ongoing meetings for implementing new business.
Client Inquiries and Escalations:
Respond to benefit plan inquiries and resolve raised and complex issues from Plan Sponsors, Plan Advisors/Consultants, and internal contacts.
Lead the unique demands of each customer, ensuring thorough and timely responses.
Service Resolution:
Champion successful outcomes for all involved, handling and negotiating service critical issues.
Advocate for favorable conclusions for all parties involved.
Amendment Requests:
Initiate amendment requests, ensuring information is complete and outstanding requirements are obtained timely.
Collaborate with Customer Experience/Operations teams to facilitate information-sharing and identify key issues.
Internal Initiatives and Meetings:
Assist with general internal initiatives related to Group Benefits services, products, and procedures.
Facilitate multiple meetings with Plan Advisors/Consultants, Plan Sponsors, and internal teams.
Take on special projects and research assignments for client needs.
Advise – 35%
Relationship Building:
Build and maintain successful and lasting relationships with existing and potential partners.
Understand the intricacies and complexities of each Plan Sponsor's Group Benefits designs and the sophistication of the Advisors/Consulting houses.
Client Reporting and Analysis:
Prepare, analyze, and interpret client reports.
Generate and compare client reports using web analytics platforms to ensure client needs are met.
Digital Expertise:
Provide expertise on Manulife’s digital experience for group benefits clients (web, mobile, voice).
Highlight key features and functionality, driving adoption of digital tools and experiences among plan members.
Provide reporting on digital uptake/usage to help clients understand overall plan health and identify areas for education/engagement.
Plan Design and Administration:
Provide recommendations on plan design, service options, technology solutions, and ongoing administration needs.
Apply understanding of legislative and privacy guidelines in client meetings.
Educate – 30%
Presentations and Training:
Deliver presentations to employee groups to facilitate enrollment and introduce Group Benefits (in person or remote).
Meet with plan administrators to train and educate them on effective plan administration methods.
Lead customer workshops and training around product updates and new features.
Product Launches:
Collaborate with Account Executives to learn, present, and educate clients on new services and products.
Use Seismic Tools to create presentations and deliver services to clients.
Assist clients in handling new services, products, and legislative initiatives.
Coaching and Mentoring:
Employ a coaching mentality to define solutions and deliver outcomes.
What are we looking for :
Must have a valid driver’s license and a personal automobile.
Capability to resolve sensitive client needs or issues.
Passionate about nurturing positive relationships and skilled at balancing multiple priorities.
Outstanding analytical, strategic, and problem-solving thinking ability.
Ability to work independently and in a team setting, with innovative thinking and a keen eye for results.
Strong customer-facing skills, including expectation management and communication.
Experience in participating and presenting in Mid-Market Finalist Presentations.
Excellent presentation skills and the ability to influence decision-makers.
Strong technical and analytical skills using Microsoft Office products, Excel, PowerPoint, and Web Analytics platforms.
Good knowledge of underwriting concepts.
GBA designation or working towards the designation is an asset.
Validated ability to take initiative, be a strong self-manager, and display integrity.
Building and strengthening existing client and consultant/advisor relationships.
In-depth knowledge and understanding of Group Benefits products and services.

You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.

You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.

item.title: Manulife: Marketing Manager, Events and Enablement
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 20, 2025 (30 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010810
We are seeking an experienced and energetic Marketing Manager, Events and Enablement to join our Group Benefits Marketing team. This role is pivotal in leading the marketing required to deliver on events in-person and virtually for our Group Benefits sponsors, advisors and Sales team, with a keen focus on event content. This team member will also support enablement activities across our Group Benefits sales team, including partnering with our Training and Product teams to deliver compelling new storytelling and training experiences, and supporting the associated content and marketing required to bring these experiences to life.
The ideal candidate is a self-starter, client-focused, creative problem solver with excellent writing and messaging development skills. You have a positive, can-do attitude, a sharp understanding of sales processes, and enjoy working with sales leaders. You understand the importance of ensuring our value proposition is consistent and evolving across all our sales channels. You are highly organized, with a passion for creating memorable experiences, and a true appreciation for the value that events and enablement can play in bringing our value proposition to life. You will work closely with various stakeholders to ensure events align with our brand values and business objectives, fostering strong relationships and delivering exceptional outcomes.
Key responsibilities:
Partner with our Events, Training, and other key teams to develop and execute an events strategy that supports the goals of our Group Benefits business and Sales team, including Manulife-hosted events, conferences, and educational sessions, as well as external industry events and sponsorships as appropriate.
Understand diverse needs and lead the development of training and education assets to enable the Sales team and help them showcase our value proposition through new product launches, presentations, etc.
Manage various aspects of events with a focus on content, presentations, and on-site executive/speaker support.
Collaborate with internal teams to ensure event themes, content, imagery, and messaging aligns with overall marketing and business strategies.
Create or contribute to the creation of compelling event marketing materials, including invitations, presentations, surveys, mobile apps, post-event communications, and signage.
Work with subject matter experts, including senior leaders, to develop content and coordinate the review and approval process.
Coach internal and external presenters to help them understand the event, audience, objectives, and where necessary, support those speakers in developing their presentation skills.
Coordinate and lead project team meetings to ensure scheduling, operational, and production timelines and deliverables are met through event project lifecycles.
Monitor event performance and gather feedback for continuous improvement.
Provide post-event analysis, including detailed reports on event success and areas for improvement.
Key attributes for success:
You thrive in a high paced environment (office & onsite), are resourceful and have demonstrated ability to balance multiple/competing priorities.
Exceptionally strong written, verbal and presentation capabilities
You think big and creatively, and approach unique problems in new ways.
Comfortable working with executives
Deadline driven with strong sense of accuracy and detail.
Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills to build effective partnerships and interact with stakeholders at all level.
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Event Management, Communications, Advertising/Promotions or a related field
Minimum of 5-7 years of experience in event planning and management, preferably in a corporate or B2B environment, including a strong focus on content/messaging development
Strong project management skills with the ability to multitask and manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with diverse teams and stakeholders.
Highly creative, out-of-the-box strategic thinker with a keen eye for detail.
Team player with exceptional organizational skills.
Flexibility to travel and work irregular hours as needed for event execution.
What can we offer you?
Competitive compensation
Retirement Savings Accounts, including a RPP (Pension Plan), RRSP (Retirement Savings Plan), and TFSA (Tax Free Savings account)
Manulife Share Ownership Program with employer matching
Customizable benefits package, including health, dental, vision, and 100% of mental health expenses up to 12K.
Financial support for ongoing training, learning, and education
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance
Wearing jeans to work every day
An abundance of career paths and opportunities to advance
