item.title: Manulife: Senior Financial Analyst, Financial Reporting & Control
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 3, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010552
The role of the Finance function is to support Manulife Bank in all financial aspects. The Finance area is responsible for financial reporting, capital management, planning & expense budgeting, benchmarking, cost analysis, accounting policy and internal controls.
Reporting to the Director, External Reporting & Capital Management, the Senior Financial Analyst will be responsible for the coordination of internal controls within Bank Finance, coordination of the annual audit engagement, committee representative for Bank Finance (records retention, etc.), and quarterly reporting to Group Finance. Additionally, the incumbent will provide support for the Bank’s quarterly and annual external reporting requirements and ad hoc financial projects, including the preparation of Manulife Bank of Canada and Manulife Trust Company financial statements, notes and regulatory disclosures, capital reporting, and ad hoc projects and tasks as required. In addition, the incumbent will review accounting pronouncements and perform an impact assessment for Manulife Bank and Manulife Trust. This position offers an opportunity for the incumbent to work with individuals across the Bank, develop well-rounded banking knowledge, and financial knowledge through the preparation of financial reporting and internal control processes.
Position Responsibilities
Financial Controls
Coordinate internal controls for Bank Finance by assessing control gaps, developing processes, and documenting procedures.
Develop and coordinate SOX significant processes for the Bank, liaise with internal and external auditors for both the Bank and Manulife Trust Company, and manage annual external audit engagement.
Financial & Capital Reporting
Provide support for preparation of the Bank’s quarterly Board meeting materials, annual financial statements, and regulatory disclosures including Pillar 3 report, monthly/quarterly capital calculations and regulatory submissions.
Provide support in reviewing new accounting pronouncements, drafting accounting policy memos, and conducting impact assessments for Manulife Bank and Trust, in collaboration with the Director and Manager of External Reporting.
Participate in financial and capital reporting project working groups, prepare dashboards for monitoring, assist in strategic presentations for senior management and external auditors, and handle special projects as required.
Attend monthly/quarterly Canadian Bankers Association (CBA) committee meetings, including the Accounting Policy Specialists Group, and research and resolve financial reporting issues as assigned
Required Qualifications:
Undergraduate degree (business, economics or finance preferred)
3-5 years of experience within a finance organization
Accounting Designation (CPA, CA, CMA, CGA)
Strong knowledge of IFRS accounting
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Preferred Qualifications:
Strong interpersonal skills, including the ability to build relationships both among internal and industry peers to keep abreast of current issues within the industry
Strong teamworking and collaboration skills
Ability to handle multiple, complex tasks concurrently
Strong computer skills in Microsoft Office applications
Demonstrates emerging ability to draft managerial reporting narratives.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Generative AI Project Manager
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 20, 2025 (30 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010805
Reporting to the Director, Generative AI Program Delivery, the Generative AI Project Manager is a consultant-minded professional who can support the delivery of our global Generative AI priorities, obsess about customers, and is excited to deliver on a highly visible, strategic transformation for our business. As a member of the Global Marketing Strategy & Transformation team, the desired candidate will help manage and implement Manulife’s multi-year Generative AI program, a major transformation program that aims to set up our organization to capture the value promised from this capability, guided by a responsible framework, with the goal of delivering significant business benefits.
The role will work with Global and Segment Gen AI Working Groups, Segment Advanced Analytics Leads, and the Global Transformation Portfolio team to ensure the Gen AI Program follows the global frameworks and guardrails that enable our businesses to measure value, identify risks, and anticipate questions and obstacles. This individual will work with a matrixed project team, directly collaborating on project governance artifacts and streamlining project management activities by navigating issues and risks in an optimized way. The individual must effectively and regularly communicate with key GenAI stakeholders.
To be successful in the role, the candidate must be outcomes-oriented, will possess demonstrated experience in delivering a highly visible program that brings in emerging technology/capabilities into a global organization, and have the experience in developing business cases to drive change as a team. This individual must also be comfortable delivering either Waterfall or Agile projects. Experience in delivering analytics/AI projects is an asset.
Key Accountabilities:
Program Governance & Coordination
Support the governance and execution of the Gen AI program in collaboration with multiple functional and technology stakeholders
Manage a single program view of multiple Gen AI initiatives
Works with Segment Working Groups and other Program teams to coordinate multiple reporting submissions to prepare a portfolio view for senior executive updates
Tracks that workstreams in the overall Gen AI program are completed within budgets and schedules while meeting business objectives
Anticipates and proposes opportunities to optimize program spend and accelerate results
Anticipates, identifies, analyzes, mitigates, documents, and controls program risks
Works with the Director, Generative AI Program Delivery to capture lessons learned and opportunities to continuously improve overall program delivery
Stakeholder Engagement & Communications
Assist in the coordination of efforts of the Gen AI Product Owner, Advanced Analytics Teams, Architects and Engineers to meet business and technical requirements
Leads the timely collection of initiative updates and executive narratives to support effective and regular communication within the project team, stakeholders, sponsors, and management
Offers guidance to Segment Working Groups and other Program teams on creating high-quality, executive-level reports and updates
Develop program status updates, findings, and/or recommendations to GenAI key stakeholders
Financial Management & Value Measurement
Lead the day-to-day execution of the Gen AI program expense management and benefits tracking processes in collaboration with the Transformation Portfolio team
Work with the Director, Generative AI Program Delivery to support the annual budget process, ensuring that Gen AI priorities, scope and budget needs are incorporated into the annual planning process
Work with Finance and/or Business partners to ensure expenses and benefits are correctly identified and tagged
Business Case Development
Lead and drive the project plan for the business case development process
Assist in the planning, mobilization and stakeholder engagement required to build the business case for Gen AI execution
Ensure the business case is developed in alignment with the enterprise-wide delivery framework, including but not limited to cost-benefit analysis, risk assessment guidelines, etc.
Required Qualifications:
Minimum 5 years of hands-on experience in business program/project management and execution
Has expertise in managing both waterfall and agile project methodologies
Ability to communicate effectively in both technical and non-technical terms, verbal and written, to both a Senior Executive audience and project delivery teams; able to effectively articulate the business opportunity, solution vision, possibilities, and outcomes to technical and non-technical audience
Demonstrates strong interpersonal skills; has experience in collaborating and partnering with business/functional stakeholders, data scientists, data engineers, architects, and vendor SMEs across a global, distributed environment
Proven track record of effective monitoring and reporting program updates, risks and escalations
High standard of work with keen attention to detail and deliverable quality
Some experience in financial analysis and financial management practices
Growth mindset; constantly learns from successes and failures
Preferred Qualifications:
PMP or CSM designation
Experience in supporting large-scale analytics/AI projects involving cross-functional technical teams, business partners, and change management partners
Knowledge of corporate function processes and capabilities
Experience in building Executive-level reports and presentations
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Bilingual PlanRight Support Agent
item.description:
locations
Waterloo, Ontario
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 8, 2025 (18 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24121525
Summary
Our PlanRight Support team assists members who have Group Retirement plans with transactions and questions on their investments, such as, fund direction and fund changes, investment management fees, rates of return and the impact of fund rationalizations. The teams operating hours are between 9:00 am to 5:00 pm, Monday to Friday, 37.5 hours per week.
Position Responsabilities:
Handle inbound, outbound and email databases to provide education to members seeking some form of guidance or desire for advice
Gather and accurately record all customer information required to meet compliance and privacy needs within CRM tool
Providing customer experience accurately and professionally
Ability to position full value of advice in order to make successful referrals to the Wealth & Retirement advisors
Ability to triage existing lead to effectively handle immediate needs or position a warm introduction to Wealth & Ret advisor, as needed
Ability to process service-related needs for clients
Meeting productivity and quality objectives established by the department as well as call audits
Meet service level agreements on activities related to Welcome calls
Proactively staying abreast of changes in legislation, product, and administrative processes
Handle advisor toll free line
Required Qualifications
Post-secondary education in finance, business or related field
1-2 years of Financial Services experience, preferably in phone
Canadian Securities Course or IFIC is an asset
Preferred Qualifications :
Passion for helping people on a one-on-one basis
Advanced listening and customer service skills
Ability to consult with all customers
Ability to adapt to the evolving needs of the business
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Background in a financial services industry
Understanding of Investment products available to Canadian Investors (eg. RRSP, RRIF)
Knowledge of segregated funds

item.title: Manulife: Learning, Talent and Development System Enablement Lead
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Boston, Massachusetts
Waterloo, Ontario
Montreal, Quebec
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 1, 2025 (11 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24110394
The Employee Experience Design Global HR team at Manulife is committed to creating exceptional experiences for our colleagues. We focus on understanding employee needs, simplifying complexities, and enabling personalized, curated experiences that matter most.
This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to the development and enhancement of our learning and talent management capabilities. The successful candidate will work with advanced technology solutions and collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive impactful results.
Reporting to VP of Employee Experience Design, your mission will be to deliver innovative technology solutions across our Talent and Development CoE in alignment with the broader HR strategy. You partner with the Employee Experience Delivery team, the Group Functional IT team and business stakeholders to support technology projects and drive continuous improvement.
Position Responsibilities:
Act as an Enablement Lead for learning, development and talent systems, ensuring alignment with the Talent and Development Center of Excellence.
Provide technical consultative support and strong business analysis to design and implement strategic solutions.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to collect business requirements, develop, test, and deploy solutions that enhance learning and talent management.
Facilitate privacy impact assessments, risk reviews and participate in vendor release activities, including assessing feature impact and assisting with testing.
Influence and communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels to drive project success.
Understanding of GenAI technologies, including model development, API integration, and deployment
Ability to analyze and interpret data to inform L&D technology strategies and measure the effectiveness of technology initiatives.
Familiarity with the latest L&D technologies and platforms to create scalable and personalized learning experiences
Ability to anticipate future skill requirements and develop technology strategies to enable the L&D strategy around upskilling and reskilling
Leading initiatives that leverage GenAI to create innovative learning solutions and drive continuous improvement.
Work effectively with cross-functional teams, including HR, IT, and business leaders, to align L&D initiatives with organizational goals.
Staying agile and responsive to the rapidly changing technological landscape, HR industry trends and best practices to continuously improve learning and talent systems.
Utilize agile methodologies and work within self-managed technology teams to deliver high-quality solutions.
Participate in the quarterly and yearly HR Technology Book of Work planning efforts in partnership with HR Strategy and the Center of Excellence. You will help influence the technology roadmap by understanding and articulating business needs to drive and evaluate/set roadmap priorities.
Partner with the Delivery Solutions team to develop strategic solutions to enable the strategy across COEs and HR teams.
Serve as a technology subject matter expert across Talent Management, Performance Enablement, Development and Culture domains.
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field.
5+ years of experience with Learning and Talent Management technologies in global, large-scale initiatives, across learning and talent systems such as Workday, EdCast, Cornerstone, and talent intelligence tools.
Proven experience supporting the system development lifecycle, including process re-engineering and technology implementations
Strong understanding of Learning and Talent Management processes and technologies, particularly Workday, EdCast, Cornerstone, etc.
Excellent communication, consultative and interpersonal skills.
Ability to think strategically and drive results.
Prior experience working on agile or self-managed technology teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Human Centered Design Certification, CHRP/CHRL, or PMP Certification is an asset.
Experience in insurance, financial, or highly regulated environments, with global experience preferred.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Data Consultant
item.description:
locations
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 14, 2025 (24 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010628
Join our Global Data and Compliance team!
How will you contribute:
Collaborating with the Director of Metrics and Analytics Shared Service;
You will be a part of a team passionate about achieving results, championing the team’s goals and embracing the enterprise’s values and objectives.
This role contributes to the development and maturation of metric automation and data analytic efforts for Global Compliance; influence how data is captured, visualized and analyzed for teams all across Global Compliance. This role will have multi-functional responsibilities that include interacting with a range of collaborators, from fellow team members to business unit partners to leaders both within the function and in other functions such as Law, Government Relations, Finance, Technology, Strategy and Risk.
This is an exciting opportunity to join an innovative, rapidly maturing team with a growth mindset.
This is a hybrid position with three days in office per week, preferably at the Waterloo, ON, Canada location.
Responsibilities:
Develop, maintain and update relevant dashboards and other data analytic tools and technologies that support partner efficiency, reporting, analysis and risk informed decision-making.
Proactively enhance data analytic and general technology and technological skills within the Compliance teams
Independently identify, analyze and define material, unmet business needs, issues, trends and opportunities within the Compliance function and recommend potential data analytic or other technology solutions.
Actively participate in projects and initiatives.
Partner, coordinate and collaborate with team members, partners and leaders within the LCG function and in other functions like Finance, Technology, Strategy and Risk.
Assist in identifying, planning and implementing change and/or continuous improvement opportunities and associated dependencies and/or roadblocks, with recommended options for addressing them.
Find opportunities to use AI in achieving function’s goals and objectives.
Skills and Experience:
Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Economics, Math, Computer Science, Engineering; or equivalent work experience.
1-3 years of progressive analytics and technology architecture experience with expertise including cloud-based technologies (Azure, Synapse), big data, data lakes, master data management, advanced data analytics for data gathering, storing and retrieval methods on various data platforms, both structured and unstructured.
Basic Working knowledge of programming languages and concepts (e.g. R, Python).
Familiarity with basic statistical tests, distributions, maximum likelihood estimators, statistical modelling techniques (regression, cluster / factor / principal components, decision tree, survival analysis, etc.)
Basic understanding of different techniques and knowledge of machine learning algorithms.
Basic knowledge of AI toolkits.
Expert in data visualization tools such as Power BI.
Expert in common database querying languages such as SQL.
Capability of applying a systematic, structured approach to explore / navigate / mine substantial amount of data, structured or un-structured, to uncover hidden patterns / insights.
Comfort working in a complex data environment with a wide variety of systems and numerous internal organizations.
Outstanding analytic and problem-solving skills, using data and information to highlight key themes and make recommendations for a senior executive audience.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our distributed team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment:
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all stakeholders and has a positive social and environmental impact.

item.title: Manulife: Director, Business Operations, Global Real Estate Operations
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
Boston, Massachusetts
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: January 26, 2025 (5 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24120940
Supporting regional offices across Canada, the United States, and Asia, the Global Real Estate (“GRE”) Operations team is accountable for developing and maintaining global policies and standards, tools and managing global processes and platform reporting, providing operational scale consistency and value for our regional investment teams.
The Director, Business Operations, within the GRE Operations team is responsible for efficiently developing, managing and communicating business unit operational policies and procedures, compliance and risk management programs, contract management and business continuity plans. The role reports to the Managing Director, Global Real Estate Operations, and actively coordinates with all functional groups within our business.
Manulife Investment Management (“MIM”) is the global wealth and asset management segment of Manulife Financial Corporation. MIM's global real estate platform provides investment solutions globally as part of its comprehensive private markets’ capabilities, offering core, core-plus, and value-add strategies.
MIM’s GRE team leverages its global platform and regional market knowledge to identify opportunities and drive results for its clients. As of September 30, 2024, its sustainably operated platform includes 90 million square feet of office, industrial, retail, and multifamily assets located across Canada, the United States, and the Asia Pacific region, with over $25 billion CAD of real estate (500+ properties). The team is expanding its capabilities to meet evolving market opportunities, building a strong and diverse global culture as an entrepreneurial investment manager.
Develop, own and lead implementation of GRE operational policies and procedures.
Act as owner and subject matter expert on leasing policies, procurement policies, playbooks, and templates. Maintain an inventory of business until policies, procedures, and templates. Track timing and refresh dates of the policies, ensuring that policy owners are keeping policies up to date.
Lead third-party manager oversight programs (e.g., annual tenant engagement survey, property management and leasing broker scorecards etc.)
Develop and lead a process related to onboarding and offboarding of investments.
Develop and lead a process related to onboarding of new ventures and funds. This includes development of a fund operational playbook and aligning accountabilities with the various functions within the business unit.
Find opportunities to build efficiencies in operational processes and support change.
Manage business unit compliance and risk management programs.
Responsible for collecting, organizing, and maintaining all business unit managed contracts, including master agreements, service contracts, and vendor agreements ensuring they are all easily accessible.
Overall lead for response to internal audits on behalf of the GRE platform.
Coordinate and oversee the external audit process as necessary.
Responsible for the administration of the AML/ATF program within GRE to ensure compliance with all applicable internal MFC policies and external regional, federal, and state laws, regulations, and industry best practices (AML onboarding screening, monitoring and reporting requirements)
Organize compliance reporting programs such as – anti-money laundering, privacy assessments and fraud risk assessments.
Identify continuous improvement opportunities to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the AML/TF program.
Lead coordination of business unit managed contracting process and contract management. This includes working with key stakeholders to identify critical business processes, assess risks, and develop strategies for mitigating disruptions.
Ensure the business managed procurement policy is followed by the GRE team and is adhering to the company’s third-party risk management standard. Train users on the standard and act as subject matter expert.
Own and manage contracting and relationship with certain third-party vendor relationships.
Develop business cases and obtain consensus for new contract types.
Responsible for collecting, organizing, and maintaining all business unit managed contracts, including master agreements, service contracts, and vendor agreements ensuring they are all easily accessible.
Maintains a centralized contract database or inventory system, where all relevant details of procurement contracts are recorded, including contract terms, renewal dates and pricing.
Tracks contract renewal and expiry dates, proactively managing the process to ensure that the contract owners are renewing contracts timely or renegotiating rates as necessary.
Responsible for receiving, reviewing, and processing invoices from vendors and suppliers in a timely and accurate manner. This involves verifying the accuracy of invoices, matching them to purchase orders and contracts, and ensuring that all necessary approvals are obtained before payment.
Business unit owner of the business continuity plan.
This includes working with key collaborators to identify critical business processes, assess risks, and develop strategies for mitigating disruptions.
Ensure that all employees are familiar with the business continuity plan, understand their roles and responsibilities during a crisis, and are prepared to implement the plan effectively.
Knowledge/Skills/Competencies/Education:
Bachelor’s Degree in business, finance, accounting, or real estate management preferred.
Minimum 10 years of experience, with minimum 5 year of experience within the investment management industry.
Ability to work independently and prioritize deadlines.
Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel. Experience with real estate accounting systems required, experience with Yardi, VTS and Anaplan a plus.
Strong knowledge of real estate investment, asset management, accounting, and administration concepts. Experience working with real estate funds a plus.
Ability to manage working with a range of collaborators to achieve objectives and deadlines. Ability to work as a team player and to develop strong working relationships across various groups and locations.
Excellent influencing and communication skills. Sound analytical and problem-solving skills. Effective customer service and interpersonal skills. Strong organizational, time management and priority setting skills to meet tight deadlines.
Strong analytical skills (both quantitative and qualitative). Strong risk assessment skills. Ability to compile, monitor, examine, and audit various financial, performance, and/or data reports for accuracy, integrity, and conformance to performance benchmarks. Strong organizational and prioritization skills.
Ability to juggle competing priorities and work under pressure to meet demanding response times, all while paying strict attention to detail.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
Manulife is an Equal Opportunity Employer

We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. item.description:
locations
USA, Massachusetts, Boston, 200 Berkeley Street
time type
Full time
posted on

time left to apply

job requisition id
JR25010529
John Hancock's Long-Term Care division is looking for a Senior Business Analyst to support emerging work in its Anti-Fraud, Waste and Abuse Program. This team focuses on implementing customer-facing fraud controls that won't negatively impact customer experience and generate savings.
We are looking for someone to perform complex research and analysis of small to medium business initiatives as well as identify, document, and analyze complex processing, service and/or technical improvement opportunities. Business initiatives may also include implementations of new technologies and new product implementations.
Working within LTC Customer Care Transformation, you will be applying agile methodologies, you will work in a Team-of-Team structure working closely with IT, Advanced Analytics, Human Center Design and Legal to optimize the LTC inforce business by achieving savings targets.
Position Responsibilities
Collaborate with multiple IT partners to Translate design, document, refine, deliver and play point on Business requirements for our document analytics solutions and LTC Storefront customer facing solutions.
Perform scrum master duties with regards to leading certain ceremonies and removing technical blockers for the team
Collaborate with actuaries on reporting and tying customer behavior to fraud waste and abuse savings.
Play the role of translator connecting the opportunity of advanced analytics capabilities to identify and prevent fraud, waste and abuse.
Required Qualifications:
3-5 years of experience in a related field, preferably financial services institution.
Comfortability with reporting or past experience with development process of software.
Background in an Agile work environment.
Experience writing, monitoring, and owning the requirements process. Taking initiative and following through is key.
Preferred Qualifications:
Salesforce Admin Experience preferred.
Long-term care industry knowledge is a plus.

item.title: Manulife: Mobile Engineering Manager
item.description:
locations
Waterloo, Ontario
CAN, Ontario, Toronto, 250 Bloor Street East
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 14, 2025 (24 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24101259
Join our Group Benefits Technology Team!
Our team is passionate about crafting seamless and innovative experiences within the Manulife Mobile application to meet the evolving needs of our users. We are currently seeking a dedicated Mobile Engineering Manager to lead our team of iOS and Android engineers.
In this role, you will drive the development of new integrations and enhance the performance and stability of Mobile app, specifically for our Group Benefits and Canadian Retirement plan members.
How will you create impact?
Collaborating with the Director of Engineering; you will work closely with the mobile team, product managers, and other multi-functional teams. This role plays a part in shaping the user experience and functionality of the Manulife Mobile application. You will contribute to the ongoing success and growth of our mobile platform, directly impacting our user base and overall business objectives. The work of this role will help drive innovation and ensure that our mobile applications remain at the forefront of technology and user satisfaction.
Responsibilities:
Leadership and Team Management:
Lead and manage a team of mobile engineers, including hiring, mentoring/coaching, performance management, and setting goals/expectations.
Foster a collaborative and engaging team environment through effective communication and team-building activities.
Identify and address skill gaps within the team by providing training, mentorship, and opportunities for professional development to ensure ongoing skill enhancement and career growth.
Supervise the on-call team, providing leadership and acting as the primary contact for critical issues to ensure timely and effective resolution.
Design and Development Oversight:
Oversee the design, development, and maintenance of the Manulife Mobile Android and iOS applications.
Ensure alignment to industry practices, coding standards, and design patterns to maintain high-quality code.
Review and approve code changes, ensuring constructive feedback is provided to maintain code quality and consistency.
Mobile Security:
Ensure compliance with industry security standards and regulations.
Oversee the utilization of encryption techniques to protect sensitive data both at rest and in transit.
Conduct regular security audits to identify and mitigate potential security risks.
Collaborate with security teams to address vulnerabilities raised through penetration testing and ensure ongoing security improvements.
Project Management:
Facilitate agile ceremonies such as sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives.
Assess and prioritize work based on urgency, impact, and alignment with organizational goals.
Efficiently allocate resources and assign tasks to team members, ensuring optimal use of skills and expertise.
Monitor team workload and capacity to ensure balanced distribution of tasks and prevent burnout.
Incident Management:
Lead the incident management process, including identification, prioritization, and resolution of production issues.
Coordinate and communicate with relevant partners during incident resolution, ensuring timely updates and transparency.
Oversee investigations into root cause analysis for major incidents and drive corrective actions to prevent recurrence.
Technical Guidance:
Lead the team to deliver on non-functional requirements and acquire necessary approvals on documentation (architecture, risk approvals, support handovers).

Direct the management and enhancement of observability tools and platforms, ensuring they deliver actionable insights and facilitate proactive incident management.
Lead performance tuning initiatives by analyzing system performance metrics and logs to identify potential bottlenecks, performance issues, and areas for improvement, driving optimal system efficiency and reliability.

Experience in leading and managing a team of engineers.
Proven experience in building native mobile applications either on iOS or Android.
Strong understanding of mobile security best practices and experience implementing security features.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and collaboration skills.
Proven track record of successfully delivering mobile applications from concept to deployment.
Experience working in an agile development environment.

item.title: Manulife: Associate, Asset Management, Real Estate Equity
item.description:
locations
Boston, Massachusetts
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 15, 2025 (25 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24071163
Manulife Investment Management (“MIM”) is the global wealth and asset management segment of Manulife Financial Corporation. MIM's global real estate (“GRE”) platform provides investment solutions globally as part of its comprehensive private markets’ capabilities, offering core, core-plus, and value-add strategies.
MIM’s GRE team leverages its global platform and regional market knowledge to identify opportunities and drive results for its clients. As of December 31, 2024, its sustainably operated platform includes 90 million square feet of office, industrial, retail, and multifamily assets located across Canada, the United States, and the Asia Pacific region, with approximately $20 billion USD of real estate (500+ properties). The team is expanding its capabilities to meet evolving market opportunities, building a strong and diverse global culture as an entrepreneurial investment manager.
MIM is seeking an Associate, US Asset Management, to play a key role within the growing platform. The Asset Management team is responsible for creating and driving the business plans for assets across the portfolios of MIM’s clients. The Associate is responsible for complex financial modeling and analysis, production of investment memorands, and supporting asset management and transactions teams. The Associate will work closely with an Asset Manager on leasing, property management, capital projects, and valuations, while gaining exposure to the Transactions and Capital Markets teams through dispositions and refinancings of assets within the region. This role requires an individual who can bring enthusiasm each day to the workplace and demonstrates an ability to handle workload management and can perform during high pressure situations related to deadlines. The position reports directly to a Senior Director, US Asset Management.
Position Responsibilities:
Provide financial analysis and support for asset-level decision-making and transactions
Conduct quantitative and qualitative analyses and reporting across the region’s portfolio, delivering forward-thinking analysis to support decision-making and strategy.
Conduct and/or support analysis for evaluation of leasing opportunities, capital projects, tenant credit and dispositions and refinancings. Develop and present findings for Senior Management, Portfolio Managers and Investment Committee.
Assist with acquisitions and dispositions when applicable.
Play an integral role in identifying market trends and extracting insights for the regional portfolio, contributing to market commentary produced by regional Asset Managers. Present and review with Senior Management and Portfolio Managers as required.
Review and validate Yardi quarterly financials/operating statements and variance reports. Understand cash flow requirements and distribution analysis
Support ongoing loan compliance and credit/security monitoring activities.
Support asset-level business plans and valuation models
Support Asset Manager coordination, production, and presentation of annual business plans to Senior Management and Portfolio Managers.
Build and maintain quarterly Argus models and support hold vs. sell analyses conducted by regional Asset Managers. Undertake model audits, quality assurance reviews, and support best practices compliance.
Coordinate appraisal review and commentary by regional Asset Managers and conduct variance analysis to external appraisals.
Support Asset Manager in oversight of leasing, property management, and capital projects
Manage the collaboration with leasing brokers, including the review of LOIs, leasing documents, and outside broker listing agreements.
Review and selectively audit asset-level financial reporting submitted from Property Managers, including capital commitment forecasts, expense recovery calculations, and funding projections. Oversee timeliness of Yardi financial and accounting information submitted from Property Managers.
Support special projects and conduct ad-hoc analyses as required.
Work with MIM business leaders to communicate effectively both internally and externally.
Serve as a mentor to analysts and contributes to a positive team culture.
Qualifications:
BA is required, preferably in finance, accounting, real estate finance or business administration, or sufficient work experience. MBA or Master’s Degree with concentration in Commercial Real Estate is preferred.
4+ years of relevant experience is required.
In-depth knowledge of real estate investment, asset management, accounting and administration concepts.
Ability to integrate top-down and bottom-up perspectives into investment recommendations.
Capable of managing competing priorities and maintaining strong attention to detail under deadlines.
Superior analytical and critical thinking skills (both quantitative and qualitative).
Able to successfully work in a team-oriented environment and partner with different functional groups.
Demonstrated leadership skills and a strong sense of ownership.
Strong work ethic, a high level of integrity, and the desire to take initiative.
Excellent written and oral communication skills. Effective customer service and interpersonal skills. Strong organizational, time management and priority setting skills.
Focused and self-motivated teammate who balances independent thinking with the execution capabilities and core proficiencies of the rest of the organization.
Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel (financial modeling, creation of templates) and Argus Enterprise. Experience with real estate accounting systems required, experience with Yardi a plus.
Some travel may be required.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words. As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.
About Manulife and John Hancock

Boston, Massachusetts

Hybrid

$91,000.00 USD - $165,000.00 USD

The actual salary will vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training. item.title: Manulife: Senior Director, Health Transformation
item.description:
locations
Hong Kong
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 11, 2025 (20 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010816
Senior Director, Health Transformation
Manulife Asia is seeking a dynamic and experienced Health Transformation Director to join our Strategy and Transformation team. The Health Transformation Director will play a pivotal role in the end-to-end execution of our Asia health strategy to drive towards our health ambition. This role requires a strategic thinker with strong project management skills, healthcare and health insurance expertise, and the ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
Position Responsibilities:
1. Strategic Execution and Leadership -
Lead the implementation of Manulife’s Asia health strategy, ensuring alignment with overall strategic priorities and Manulife’s health ambitions.
Develop and maintain a comprehensive health strategy execution plan, clearly defining roles, responsibilities, tasks, and key milestones for health-focused initiatives.
Facilitate the development of key health metrics and indicators to track the success of initiatives, ensuring alignment with strategic health value drivers and objectives.
2. Project Management and Governance -
Oversee and manage the execution of the plan, ensuring projects are delivered on time and within scope, through effective governance to maintain momentum and accountability.
Facilitate the health steering committee to ensure strategic alignment and oversight from executive stakeholders.
Identify, manage, and escalate key issues and risks in a timely manner, ensuring proactive problem-solving and mitigation strategies.
3. Insights Sharing and Opportunities Assessment -
Continuously assess the health landscape to identify opportunities for innovation and improvement, recommending actions to accelerate progress towards our health ambition and outperform peers.
Provide expert insights and guidance leveraging industry knowledge and analytical skills to steer towards successful outcomes.
4. Program Success and Culture Building -
Elevate the profile of health initiatives within the organization, ensuring they receive the necessary focus and resources.
Motivate and encourage health-focused workstream leads, fostering a collaborative and high-performance culture.
Recognize and celebrate successes within the health strategy program to maintain morale and highlight positive outcomes.
Required Qualifications:
Extensive experience in the healthcare or health insurance industry, with a strong understanding of market dynamics and regulatory environments in Asia.
1. Project management expertise:
Proven track record in project management, with the ability to manage complex, multi-market projects and drive them to successful completion.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
2. Strategic thinking
Sound knowledge of the health insurance business and healthcare landscape.
A deep experience in health insurance products and operations, and ideally an understanding of key Manulife systems and processes.
Strong strategy background and analytical capability, ability to translate into action items which drive initiatives, including data-driven problem solving and decision-making skills.
Solid financial acumen to evaluate and understand financially feasible actions for strategy implementation.
3. Leadership
Strong business acumen with proven experience in stakeholder management.
Ability to positively engage with various stakeholders and supports, advocates, communicates and implement change.
Collaborative mindset with exceptional stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate with senior leaders and cross-functional teams.
Excellent presentation and communication skills.
Self-motivated, independent and with excellent interpersonal skills
4. Education and qualification
University degree or higher, with preference to degrees in healthcare, medicine, business administration or a related field.
Fluency in English is required; proficiency in one or more Asian languages is an advantage.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.

Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)
Retirement savings benefit
Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Performance Bonus
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
Job Description:
We’re looking for a Senior Procurement Specialist to join Global Investment Product Team within Procurement Operations group at MBPS. In this role, you are oversight of the end-to-end execution of the sourcing & contracting process for various spend categories for NA sourcing initiatives driving savings targets and other organizational goals
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
Responsibilities
Conduct detailed analysis and develop relevant sourcing and negotiation strategy for assigned projects and Generate and leverage market and supplier intelligence and data for development of sourcing and negotiation strategies
Submit RFx (RFI, RFQ, RFP or e-Auction) requests to suppliers and make recommendations to the business partners on which providers will meet the commercial print or digital communication needs
Participate during the contract negotiation process, with consultative support, as required, from legal and risk
Perform other related strategic sourcing activities and participate in special projects as assigned
Support and deliver on key transformation strategies (i.e. Ivalua implementation) of the group, as a priority.

Qualifications:
Must be Bachelor's degree holder College/University graduate
5+ years of experience in strategic sourcing and/or global supply chain related to commercial print or digital communication
Strong experience with sourcing and procurement systems and tool and Technical and professional knowledge – demonstrated understanding and application of procurement and contracting principles and best practices
Excellent analytical skills with the ability to develop complex cost model and come up with actionable cost-optimization recommendations
In-depth knowledge of sourcing and procurement principles and best practices
Advance knowledge in Excel (Pivot, Vlookup, Concatenate)
Amenability and readiness to work onsite and from home anytime (dependent on business need AND/OR current external environment/situation)
Willing to work in on night shift schedule

item.title: Manulife: Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 10, 2025 (19 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24090704
The opportunity
Does change excite you? Do you want to be part of the team that redefines how we get work done? We are changing the way we manage technology and we want you to be part of it! We are growing our teams, with the mandate to power world-class customer and employee experiences through software and we need you.
We are seeking a self-motivated engineer, who is obsessed with understanding the business, delivering value, are forward-thinking, and excited to see the successful implementation of the products they deliver for the Wealth and Asset Management business at Manulife. The team are part way through implementing a major digital system that will standardize our investment processes across Asia. This role will handle the implementation of the digital systems from front to back for Manulife Investment Management in the Philippines.

Responsibilities
Build, code, test, maintain high quality software
Translate user requirements into software requirements
Design, develop, test, deliver, maintain and improve business applications as a member of a team, works across full-stack through entire software development lifecycle
Prioritize efficient code production and handle daily production issues, if any
You will demonstrate the following behaviours:
Have a driving entrepreneurial spirit and comfort working within a rapidly changing startup environment
Love the challenge of working on a small team and being part of a larger movement to change the culture of an enterprise
Collaborate with and demonstrate empathy and generosity towards business stakeholders, product managers, software engineers, and operations engineers to uncover pain points
Be curious to understand the fundamentals of capital markets and financial services in support of our service and product offerings
Collaborative attitude, willingness to work with team members; able to coach, participate in code reviews, share skills and methods
Constantly learn from both success and failure
Good organizational and problem-solving abilities that enable you to manage through creative abrasion
Good verbal and written communication; able to effectively articulate technical vision, possibilities, and outcomes
Experiment with emerging technologies and understanding how they will impact what comes next
Managing the full Software Delivery Lifecycle including coding, testing, deployment, and support, in a DevOps environment
Translate user requirements into software requirements
Practice TDD and BDD engineering techniques
How will you create impact?

The role will report to the Head of Business Solutions.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Proficiency in javascript/typescript stack (Node.js, NestJS, React), RESTful, GraphQL/gRPC, JSON
Proficiency in API development (REST, Graph)
Experience with distributed messaging and event driven systems (Kafka, MQ, etc.)
Knowledge of relational as well as NoSQL Document Database
Exposure to Docker, Kubernetes Container Orchestration is a plus
High proficiency in developmental toolsets including CI/CD tools
Experience with MicroServices and development tools like GIT
Excellent analytical, problem solving skills
Understanding of Agile methodologies

item.title: Manulife: Senior Manager, Information Risk Management Control Assurance Testing (ICAT)
item.description:
locations
Tokyo
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 28, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24060695
The opportunity
The Senior Manager, Information Risk Management Control Assurance Testing (ICAT) will play a vital role in the 2nd Line of Defense global ICAT team responsible for assessing the information risk management activities to provide independent and objective control assurance against the company’s information risk appetite.

Key Responsibilities
Perform information risk assurance assessments and controls testing according to the company’s policies and standards, e.g. Identity and Access Management, vulnerability management, network security, application security, IT asset inventory etc.
Provide risk management services and advisory initiatives for information and technology risks
Collaborate with different lines of defense, subject matter expertise, to carry out assurance project planning and keep pace with both internal drivers (IRM standards and Technology processes) and external drivers (technology, regulations, risks, and control standards)
Ensure that all assurance procedures executed are conducted in accordance with Global IRM Methodologies
Collaborate with regional, market Information Risk Officers, Governance & Control teams to ensure process assurance of key risks and gaps identified from assurance engagements, and to define, track and report on management corrective action plans
Develop relationships with teams across the globe, regionally, and locally, e.g. IT, Audit Services, Global Information Risk Management, Operational Risk Management, and other risk and control groups to develop integrated strategies to manage our most key areas of risk
Commit to and contribute to the ongoing evolution of assurance processes, standardization of procedures, and continuous improvement
Stay abreast of evolving information and technology risks, as well as new regulations, laws, and requirements for technology, information security, cybersecurity, and privacy across the company's jurisdictions
Job Requirements
Minimum 5 years of progressive experience in the areas of Information Security / Business Resiliency / Technology Risk strategies, principles, processes, and deliverables within a large enterprise
University degree in Risk Management, Business Information Systems, Computer Science or related discipline is preferred
Relevant work experience in financial services and technology operations, e.g. information risk management, or audit function
In depth knowledge of system development methodologies, cyber and network security processes, and regulatory requirements (CA OSFI, US SOX, JP FSA, HK SFC, SG MAS etc.)
Sound knowledge of best practices of various aspects of information risk management Previous risk advisory consulting experience is preferred
Competencies
Fluency in both Japanese and English, including speaking, reading, and writing
Results oriented with a keen focus on quality and delivering value; ability to balance multiple priorities and projects; strong attention to detail while retaining focus on the “big picture” and top risks; flexible and organized with the ability to oversee multiple projects concurrently
Strong communication, stakeholder management, and presentation skills
Problem solving, analytical, innovative, and strategic thinker
Ability to work independently and effectively collaborate with teammates around the globe
Ability to quickly comprehend business processes and identify the risk implications, analyze complex situations, reach appropriate conclusions, and make value-added and practical recommendations.
Recognized professional designations in Information Security, Audit and Business Continuity (e.g., CISSP, CISA, CISM, CEH, CRISC, FAIR)
Demonstrated ability to work effectively in diverse environments and cultures, over multiple office locations
Ability to identify opportunities to utilize data analytics for enhanced depth and breadth of assurance coverage
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.

Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

item.title: Manulife: Corporate Accounts Specialist
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 10, 2025 (19 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24120680
The opportunity
The Corporate Account Specialist is our main support the after sales transactions of our Agency Channel. The main accountability is to achieved a policy and premium persistency of 85%, generate billings on time (initial, adjustment, modal, renewal, extension), ensure accurate member data information are saved in the database, confirm member eligibility for claims verification, provide details for account review, process experience and unused premium refunds, and perform member underwriting for members exceeding the NEL/NML upon renewal of the account. Closely coordinate with the Distributors and Business Partners to be able to deliver to our service level commitments to our clients.

Responsibilities
Prepare renewal offers for existing accounts and ensure to manage his/her portfolio to maintain the 85% policy and premium persistency level.
Reconcile member master list upon renewal based on updated list submitted by the client vs. our record on file. Discrepancy may require adjustment billing to include, delete or adjust the coverage of the members as applicable. Perform member underwriting within Group Admin limits for members exceeding the NEL/NML.
Provide renewal details (member list and movements, premium and claims for the policy year) to pricing for account review.
Verify member eligibility for claims verification, including checking of updated premium payment and most recent approved coverage from underwriting.
Prepare billing statements such as Initial, Adjustment, Modal, Renewal and Extension Billing issued to Group Policy Holder. Billing statement’s granularity is up to individual member level.
Give timely feedback to Distribution Partners or to the clients for Direct accounts if there are discrepancies on the member data and/or coverage.
Prepare Policy Contracts, Certificate of Cover and Individual Certificate of Insurance for issued new business and renewed existing accounts, as applicable.
Conduct KYC (Know Your Customer) and AML (Anti-Money-Laundering) compliance for new business and new affiliates for existing accounts.
Set up policies/benefit schedule based on proposal accepted by the client during new business and renewal.
Prepare policy endorsements as part of the contract for non-standard contract provision, special approval from Actuarial and take-over accounts.
Tag along with Account Managers of Group Admin and Business Development Specialists of Sales Team for client meetings and during account review.
Process experience refund as applicable. Provide
Provide immediate resolution to clients and agents’ inquiries and complaints.

How will you create impact?

The role will report to the Corporate Account Team Lead.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
College graduate of any 4year course
Requires 2-3 years of Group Accounts Management experience
Excellent customer service orientation
Preferably with Basic LOMA course taken and passed
Proficiency in MS Office notably MS Excel
Team player
Continuous Improvement Orientation
Strong analytical skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.

time type
Full time
posted on

time left to apply

job requisition id

The opportunity
The Area Manager is responsible for over-all sales performance through recruitment, management, and development of all the Financial Sales Associates under the area of assignment. Furthermore, the position is the primary Relationship Officer in the area and is assigned to manage the relationship between the Financial Sales Associate and the China Bank branch officers and staff, as well as the relationship of Manulife China Bank and the China Bank Area Head.
Responsibilities
Deliver sales production targets in terms of parameters set by the company for his area of assignment.
Ensure that Financial Sales Associates in the area deliver Minimum Validation Requirement set by the company.
In charge of the Financial Sales Associate recruitment activities within the area to ensure 100% Financial Sales Associate compliment within the area.
Manage relationship within China Bank branch officers and staff and the Area Head of the designated Area.
Assist the Financial Sales Associates in gathering and converting Qualified Sales Leads within the branches in the area in order to achieve 100% branch activation.
Manage all the sales related activities of the Financial Sales Associates within the designated area through monitoring and activity management system.
Develop the selling skills and professional knowledge of each Financial Sales Associates in the area through joint field work, coaching, and training.
Generate and submit all reports requested by management timely and accurately.

item.title: Manulife: Head of Product, Sales & Distribution Compliance
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 1, 2025 (10 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24110850
The opportunity
The Distribution Compliance Team is responsible for ensuring the quality and sustainability of the business as the salesforce continues to expand. New and evolving regulations such as the Financial Products and Services Consumer Protection Act (FCPA), Manulife initiative on Conduct Risk Framework and Project Minotaur, will put significant emphasis on the organization’s ability to deliver fair and positive customer outcomes. The Distribution Compliance role is critical in providing guidance and support to the business to meet new regulatory changes and Manulife initiatives.
The job holder will be responsible for Distribution Compliance with following key responsibilities:
Subject Matter Expert (SME) for Product, Sales Conduct, distribution compliance and fair treatment of customers across the full policy lifecycle.
Management and oversight of regulatory/compliance obligations; Ensuring controls and processes are in place to detect and mitigate any risk of non-compliance.
Develop and implement the compliance monitoring and testing program for on-going assessment of control effectiveness of PSD risk.
Preparation, review and submission of internal and regulatory reporting within the timeline.
Individual Accountabilities:
Act as primary reviewer of all life insurance products.
Work with L1 to identify, monitor and report PSD risk that may arise to the Chief Compliance Officer (CCO).
Develop and conduct risk assessment models, tools and controls testing to identify risks and propose the control measure for mitigation approach.
Carry out annual compliance quality assurance testing in accordance with the Distribution Compliance Controls Testing Guide, provision of law, internal rules and regulation and applicable professional ethical standards applicable to Manulife and MCBL sales force.
Oversight the implementation of controls (QA, sample testing, mystery shopping, etc.) by 1LOD, review the testing results and advise on the mitigation actions.
Ensure that the complaints, allegations, indicators (from various sources) relating to agent sale misconduct are properly and timely assessed to provide fair feedback to customer and for appropriate disciplinary action imposed on the violation agents.
Ensure that the sales misconduct & distribution fraud investigation are conducted, including progression of activities or steps moving from evidence gathering tasks, data/information analysis, to theory development and validation, to forming reasonable ground to believe, to interview directly the related parties and finally to enforce ethic code timely.
Review the sales & marketing documents to ensure regulatory compliance.
Review appropriateness of sale compensation structure, etc. ensure that no Salespeople are incentivized based on compensation and incentive programs that favor certain products or features over the interests of clients.
Conduct TPDD on the sale partners as company requirements
Main coordinator for the Internal Audit Examination, Divisional Quality Assurance Testing and Divisional Compliance Testing.
Other tasks and projects as assigned by the Chief Compliance Officer.

How will you create impact?

Role will report to Chief Compliance Officer.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.

What we are looking for
Minimum 7 years relevant experience in insurance, financial services industry or regulatory bodies in Compliance.
Knowledge of financial services industry, related legislations, regulators, industry best practices and professional standards, especially in Product, Sale & Distribution Compliance.
Knowledge and experience in insurance product.
Good interpersonal skills to work with various stakeholders and as a team.
Able to work under pressure with good Compliance judgement skill.
Professional qualification in law or accounting, insurance, fraud examination is an asset.
Customer centric attitude and a sense of execution urgency.
Able to work independently and proactively, as well as be a team player. Support on local and regional projects.
Familiarity with system reports and presentations utilizing excel and PowerPoint skills.
Establish and maintain relationships with industry association and members.

What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.

item.title: Manulife: Health VAS & Partnerships Manager
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 1, 2025 (10 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24110521
The opportunity
This role will be accountable for the developing and implementing value-added services (VAS) to enhance the overall health and protection insurance offering. Collaborating with the product and propositions managers, cross-functional teams and Head of Health, you shall help develop VAS strategies and implement defined strategies by integrating value-added services into the insurance product offering. You shall identify and manage partnerships with healthcare providers, wellness programs, and other relevant service providers. You shall be accountable in monitoring and analyzing the performance of value-added services to ensure they meet customer needs and contribute significantly to the overall business objectives of the Health and Protections category. This role shall also ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and industry standards related to value-added services.

Responsibilities
Collaborating with Head of Health, product and propositions managers and helping develop strategies on VAS and partnership. This will include developing the framework for VAS and partner selection and monetization models for VAS.
Researching and identifying opportunities for value-added services that align with product strategies including the needs and preferences of the target market.
Negotiating and managing partnerships with healthcare providers, wellness programs, and other service providers to enhance the value proposition of the health and protection insurance offering.
Developing and managing the budget for value-added services and partnerships.
Collaborating with marketing and distribution teams to communicate the value of the added services to members and potential customers.
Collaborating with Customer Experience and Operation team to ensure delivery of VAS and partner services are optimized and deliver a consistently fantastic customer experience.
Providing regular reporting and analysis on the performance and impact of value-added services.

What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.

What we are looking for
Experience in the Health industry. Experience in the insurance industry and financial services is a plus.
Experience in consumer sale and/or marketing, partner development and management
Strong understanding of the healthcare industry, including current trends and best practices in value-added services.
Excellent negotiation and relationship management skills to build and maintain partnerships with service providers.
Analytical skills to assess the performance and impact of value-added services and make data-driven decisions.
Project management skills to oversee the development and implementation of new value-added services.
Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with cross-functional teams and external partners.
Graduate of bachelor degree preferably major in Marketing, Management, Business Administration, or another equivalent field.
At least 5 years working experience.

item.title: Manulife: Income Pay-out Officer
item.description:
locations
Makati City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: February 1, 2025 (10 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24120019
The opportunity
The Income-payout Officer provides administrative support to internal and external customers (policyholders, partners and distributors) who request for VUL income payout and inquiries. Ensures that customer transactions are done within turnaround time (TAT).
The role includes handling of income payout related transactions, providing resolution to any issues/discrepancies that may arise related to disbursement and adhering to established procedures and policies set by the Company.
Responsibilities:
Handling of the end to end process of income payout
Deals with policyholders and agents (client interface—oral and listening skills necessary, and through correspondence) in response to various policy inquiries and other related income payout transactions.
Able to give ready answers to inquiries on products, interest rates, policy values and other policy information.
Knows the processes and systems to be able to handle verification of details, responding to questions and clarifications.
Promptly reports any error/issues or concerns that could impact the process and request of the clients
Ensure that processes are in accordance with the company’s guidelines and procedures.
Able to attend to other transactions such as Disbursement Batch processing and Bridger Checking
Timely preparation and submission of the assigned report/s
How will you create impact?
The role will be reporting to the Client & Account Manager.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
What we are looking for
Degree Holder of any four year course
2-4 years of professional experience in the Life Insurance industry
Certificate in Life Insurance or Diploma in Life Insurance would be an advantage
Good working knowledge in MS Office Applications (i.e., Word, Excel, Power Point)
With Customer Service Mindset and Good Communication and Listening Skills

A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.

Professional development and leadership opportunities.

item.title: Manulife: Senior Software Technical Specialist - DevOps
item.description:
locations
Quezon City
Lapu Lapu City,
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: March 24, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24090796
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)
Retirement savings benefit
Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Performance Bonus
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
Job Description:
We’re looking for a Senior Software Technical Analyst to join our DevOps team at MBPS. In this role, you are expected to support and maintain critical digital wealth applications (both front-end and back-end) across several markets in Asia.
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
Key Responsibilities:
The Senior Software Technical Analyst will set up and respond to alerts from monitoring tools like New Relic and Devo, providing Level 2 analysis and troubleshooting for both front-end and back-end digital wealth applications.
They will investigate and resolve production issues using tools such as MongoDB, ServiceNow, Postman, and Swagger UI, while maintaining and supporting batch job processes.
The role requires taking ownership of incident management, including on-call duties, to ensure timely resolution of issues within SLAs.
The analyst will liaise with internal/external customers, business partners, and project teams to facilitate the transition of new enhancements into production support and maintain documentation in Confluence and Jira.
They will also consistently apply continuous improvement practices to enhance support activities and drive issue resolution with multiple stakeholders.
Qualifications:
Education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Information Systems, or any related course
Experience: Minimum 5-7 years working experience supporting systems at Level 2 support (with code level investigation)
Hybrid Work Arrangement: Hybrid (3x a week onsite)
Minimum Skills to Hire/Must Haves:
Web app knowledge (Frontend and Back End web technologies, APIs etc.)
SQL
JavaScript or any programming language knowledge (C#, VB Script, .NET / .NET Core)
React JS / Node JS (Preferred, not required)
MongoDB (Preferred, not required)
Let's make every day better together. Learn about our opportunities at JOBS.MANULIFE.COM
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.
